reasoning: 8x zoom inspection of pixel art key sprites (multiple size variants). Sub-pixel features: none — clean pixel grid throughout. AA edges: none — all hard pixel boundaries as expected for pixel art. Noise in solid areas: a few isolated stray pixels near key edges, minor. Outline consistency: outlines are 1px throughout and consistent; no missing or doubling. Shading: proper 2-3 hard tones (dark outline, mid orange/gold, bright highlight) — no gradients detected. Pattern artifacts: none. Melting details: smallest scale variants are appropriately sparse for their pixel budget. Asymmetry: the shaft of the middle-left medium-scale variant shows slight left/right asymmetry of about 1px, acceptable for this art style. Color bleed: none observed at color boundaries. Semantic coherence: all sprites read clearly as ornate keys with handle, shaft, and bow — no structural confusion. Overall the palette is tightly controlled and the pixel grid discipline is solid. Minor issues do not rise to marginal level.
